suppress.notes: JSON View plugins are versioned independently. They are not Grails Framework 3.2.3.
Async/Events plugins are not Grails 5.0.2 Framework.
The converters plugin version 5.0.0 is misidentified as the Grails 5.0.0 framework.
Grails Profiles use separate versioning. Project is actually Grails 6.2.3.
Custom FOLIO plugin versioned 1.0.0, incorrectly flagged as Grails 1.0.0.
Grails 6 uses Spring 5.3.x. This RMI vulnerability is a known ghost hit for Spring 5. We suppress this specifically because we are not using HTTP Invoker.
Suppressing the incorrect Spring Boot 2.7.18 CPE mapping for the starter packages. This ensures we are alerted if a native vulnerability is found in the package itself.
Suppressing the incorrect Spring Boot 2.7.18 CPE mapping for the starter packages. This ensures we are alerted if a native vulnerability is found in the package itself.

CRITICAL WARNING: Suppressing CVE-2026-40975.
We COULD become vulnerable if we decide to autogenerate random secrets with ${random.value}, but we should NEVER do this in production anyway.

CRITICAL WARNING: Suppressing CVE-2026-40973 because we currently do NOT use persistent sessions.
DO NOT SET `server.servlet.session.persistent=true` IN APPLICATION.YML! If you enable persistent sessions, this suppression MUST be removed immediately, as the application will become actively vulnerable to local code execution.

CRITICAL WARNING: Suppressing CVE-2026-40977 because we currently do NOT use ApplicationPidFileWriter.
DO NOT make use of ApplicationPidFileWriter in code that can make it to production systems, as this would bring our modules into scope for this vulnerability.
Suppressing CVE-2026-40974 because we do not use Cassandra NoSQL database

CRITICAL WARNING: Suppressing CVE-2026-22733 because we do not use CloudFoundry actuator endpoint and do not have any subpaths to them requiring authentication.
It is unlikely, but be aware of CloudFoundry actuator endpoint paths.
Suppressing the incorrect Netty 4.1.101 CPE mapping for the subpackages. This should still ensure we are alerted if a native vulnerability is found in the package itself.
Suppressing the incorrect Undertow 2.2.37 CPE mapping for the subpackages. This should still ensure we are alerted if a native vulnerability is found in the package itself.
Suppressing the incorrect spring-security 5.7.11 CPE mapping for the subpackages. This should still ensure we are alerted if a native vulnerability is found in the package itself.
